Brazilian militias.description: Brazilian militias are illegal paramilitary groups composed mainly of current and former police officers, firefighters, and politicians, operating as mafia-like organizations. Based primarily in Rio de Janeiro, they engage in extortion, protection rackets, and other organized crime activities, often displacing drug gangs to control favelas. They emerged in the late 2000s from death squads rooted in the Brazilian military dictatorship. The groups maintain deep political connections and have been implicated in high-profile assassinations.

descriptions: Militias force out drug gangs like ADA from favelas to establish their own protection rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: In the favelas, drug gangs like ADA and Red Command control trafficking and violence networks... These police-backed militias historically force out the drug traffickers in order to set up their own protection rackets

Jewish-American organized crime.description: Jewish-American organized crime emerged in the late 19th and early 20th centuries within the American Jewish community. Based primarily in New York City, it was involved in bootlegging, loansharking, gambling, and other rackets. Key figures include Arnold Rothstein, who transformed organized crime into a corporate-like enterprise. The group has historical ties to Italian-American Mafia and other criminal organizations.

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with Jewish-American organized crime.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ...African-American mob, Armenian mafia, Chaldean mafia, Corsican mafia, Dixie Mafia, Greek mafia, Jewish-American mob, Lebanese mafia, Mexican Mafia, Russian mafia, Serbian mafia, various motorcycle gangs...

descriptions: The American Mafia has done business with Jewish-American organized crime.
edges.evidence_quote: has at various times done business with the Irish Mob , Jewish-American organized crime , the Japanese yakuza , Indian mafia , the Russian mafia , Thief in law and Post-Soviet Organized crime groups, the Chinese triads , Chinese Tongs and Asian street gangs, Motorcycle Gangs and numerous White, Black and Hispanic prison and street gangs.

Hook Gang.description: The Hook Gang was a street gang and river pirates active in New York City's Fourth Ward and Corlear's Hook districts from the mid-1860s until 1876. Formed after the American Civil War, the gang numbered 50-100 members and engaged in armed robbery, theft, hijacking, and river piracy. They were broken up by the New York City Police Department in 1876.

descriptions: MOD splintered from Peace MOD.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1988, a handful of Hmong youth broke off from one of the first Hmong gangs in California known as "Peace MOD", originally formed in 1985 in Fresno, California to combat other gangs preying upon the Hmong community. The splinter group formed the "Masters of Destruction" (MOD).

'Ndrina Pititto.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) based in Mileto, Vibo Valentia, Italy, involved in extortion and drug trafficking. It was established in the 1980s and led by Giuseppe Prostamo until his death. The clan has been involved in several operations and trials, including the Stammer operation in 2017 which seized 8 tons of cocaine. It remains active.

descriptions: Fiarè and Pititto-Prostamo-Iannello were both targeted in the same operation for international drug trafficking, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 25 gennaio 2017 si conclude l'operazione Stammer che chiede l'arresto di 54 persone e di cui 42 effettivamente arrestate accusate a vario titolo di associazione a delinquere, tra cui presunti affiliati dei Mancuso di Limbadi, dei Fiarè di San Gregorio d'Ippona, dei Pititto-Prostamo-Iannello di Mileto e un gruppo di San Calogero e traffico internazionale di stupefacenti.

descriptions: Mancuso and Pititto-Prostamo-Iannello were targeted together in operation Stammer, indicating cooperation.

Kansas City crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Kansas City, Missouri, formed in the early 1910s by the DiGiovanni brothers. It was led for decades by Nicholas Civella, who expanded its influence into Las Vegas casinos. The family is involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and other illegal activities, and remains active with an estimated 12 made members as of 2015.

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to the Fenkell Boys, meaning the Fenkell Boys succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

Garduña.description: The Garduña is a mythical secret criminal society said to have been founded in Spain in the late Middle Ages, around 1417. It was allegedly involved in robbery, kidnapping, arson, and murder-for-hire. However, historians have traced all references to a 19th-century book and doubt its existence. It is considered a foundational myth for Italian mafias like the Camorra and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Text notes similar founding myths for Chinese Triads, implying a parallel but not a direct relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Similar traditions are told of three Buddhist monks founding the Chinese Triads.

descriptions: Cutolo drew inspiration from the Garduna criminal association.
edges.evidence_quote: Cutolo si ispirò ... alla confraternita della garduna

descriptions: ISMP emerged from Mozambican Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: It emerged out of the Mozambican Islamist group Al-Shabaab.

Norteños.description: Norteños are a collection of affiliated gangs in California that pay tribute to Nuestra Familia while in state and federal prisons. Founded in 1968 by Mexican American inmates, they primarily operate in Northern California and are known for their rivalry with Sureños. Their membership consists mainly of Mexican Americans, and they engage in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang uses the number 14 and the color red as symbols of affiliation.

Cesarano clan.description: The Clan Cesarano is a Camorra organization originating from Ponte Persica, a hamlet northeast of Castellammare di Stabia, Italy, and operating there with strong interests and hegemony also in the neighboring municipality of Pompei. Founded by Ferdinando Cesarano, a charismatic figure who was a ruthless killer and capozona of the Nuova Famiglia in the late 1970s and early 1980s, the clan became independent after the dissolution of the Nuova Famiglia in the mid-1980s. As of 2014, the boss is Luigi Di Martino, known as 'O Profeta.

descriptions: Alfieri clan is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alfieri clan (defunct)

edges.evidence_quote: affiliati al clan Alfieri della provincia napoletana

edges.evidence_quote: The Cesaranos managed, in agreement with the D'Alessandro clan, another clan from Castellammare di Stabia, the monopoly on the funerals

descriptions: A member of Clan D'Alessandro supported Nicola Esposito in planning a murder within Clan Cesarano.
edges.evidence_quote: con l'appoggio di un esponente del clan D'Alessandro di Castellammare di Stabia

Atlantic Guards.description: The Atlantic Guards were a 19th-century American street gang active in New York City from the 1840s to the 1860s. Based in the Bowery, they were a nativist vigilante group focused on protecting Bowery neighborhoods and often warred with Irish American gangs of the Five Points, especially the Dead Rabbits. They were allies of the Bowery Boys and participated in the Dead Rabbits Riot of 1857.

Liga da Justiça.description: The largest and most well-known militia faction in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, founded in 1995-1996. Originally a paramilitary group called 'Os Caras do Posto', it operates in the West Zone of Rio, engaging in assassinations, extortion, and terrorist activities. Its leaders include Ricardo Teixeira Cruz (Batman) and Aldemar Almeida dos Santos (Robin). The group is still active.

descriptions: The two clubs engaged in a shootout at a gas station in Argentina.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 May 2016, three people were seriously injured as a result of a shootout between the Hells Angels and Los Tehuelches, a rival motorcycle gang with Neo-Nazi sympathies consisting primarily of active and former police officers, at a highway gas station in Luján, Buenos Aires.
